children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: A State is a qualifying State if the Secretary of Agriculture (hereinafter in this chapter referred to as the “Secretary”) determines that the State has in effect a mediation program that meets the requirements of subsection (c).

children.heading: Determination by Secretary
children.label: b Determination by Secretary
children.text: Within 15 days after the Secretary receives from the Governor of a State a description of the mediation program of the State and a statement certifying that the State has met all of the requirements of subsection (c), the Secretary shall determine whether the State is a qualifying State.

children.heading: Requirements of State mediation programs
children.label: c Requirements of State mediation programs

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: To be certified as a qualifying State, the mediation program of the State must provide mediation services to persons described in paragraph (2) that are involved in agricultural loans (regardless of whether the loans are made or guaranteed by the Secretary or made by a third party).

children.label: i
children.text: Wetlands determinations.

children.label: ii
children.text: Compliance with farm programs, including conservation programs and the national organic program established under the Organic Foods Production Act of 1990 ( et seq.).7 U.S.C. 6501

children.label: iii
children.text: Agricultural credit.

children.label: iv
children.text: Rural water loan programs.

children.label: v
children.text: Grazing on National Forest System land.

children.label: vi
children.text: Pesticides.

children.label: vii
children.text: Lease issues, including land leases and equipment leases.

children.label: viii
children.text: Family farm transition.

children.label: ix
children.text: Farmer-neighbor disputes.

children.label: x
children.text: Such other issues as the Secretary or the head of the department of agriculture of each participating State considers appropriate for better serving the agricultural community and persons eligible for mediation.

children.label: i
children.text: prior to the initiation of any mediation involving the Department of Agriculture; or

children.label: ii
children.text: unrelated to any ongoing dispute or mediation in which the Department of Agriculture is a party.

children.label: i
children.text: agricultural producers;

children.label: ii
children.text: creditors of producers (as applicable);

children.label: iii
children.text: persons directly affected by actions of the Department of Agriculture; and

children.label: iv
children.text: any other persons involved in an issue for which mediation services are provided by a mediation program described in paragraph (1)(B).

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Subject to clause (ii) and , a person may not be compelled to participate in mediation services provided under this Act.section 5103 of this title

children.heading: State laws
children.label: ii State laws
children.text: Clause (i) shall not affect a State law requiring mediation before foreclosure on agricultural land or property.

children.label: A
children.text: provides for mediation services that, if decisions are reached, result in mediated, mutually agreeable decisions between the parties to the mediation;

children.label: B
children.text: is authorized or administered by an agency of the State government or by the Governor of the State;

children.label: C
children.text: provides for the training of mediators;

children.label: D
children.text: provides that the mediation sessions shall be confidential;

children.label: E
children.text: ensures, in the case of agricultural loans, that all lenders and borrowers of agricultural loans receive adequate notification of the mediation program; and

children.label: i
children.text: the Department of Agriculture receives adequate notification of those issues; and

children.label: ii
children.text: persons directly affected by actions of the Department of Agriculture receive adequate notification of the mediation program.
the intake and scheduling of cases;
the provision of background and selected information regarding the mediation process;
financial advisory and counseling services (as appropriate) performed by a person other than a State mediation program mediator; and
the mediation session.
